vscode.name: VS Code Insiders

csp.notes: Policy is fixed by the host. _meta.ui.csp.connectDomains/resourceDomains are ignored; the iframe is sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' with no allow= for autoplay/microphone/camera.

server-initiated.notes: VS Code negotiates a session and supports sampling/createMessage natively (first call shows a consent dialog). The server MUST use a stateful transport (sessionIdGenerator: randomUUID); the default stateless transport silently times out server-initiated requests. Elicitation/subscriptions not yet validated firsthand.

bundling.notes: vite-plugin-singlefile inlines all JS/CSS into one HTML. External <script src> and <link href> to CDNs are blocked by CSP.

port-config.notes: HTTP on 3xxx, TLS on 4xxx. mcp.json points to TLS ports.

known-broken-patterns: Map/geo SDKs that load their script from a vendor CDN and fetch tiles or assets from vendor domains at runtime (CDN script tag + connect-src both blocked)
GL/worker-based renderers that compile styles via eval or bootstrap worker scripts through blob + eval
MVVM/templating libraries that compile data-binding strings via new Function() (string-to-code)
Media apps that point <audio>/<video> at external URLs or rely on autoplay/speechSynthesis (media-src 'self', no autoplay/web-speech grant) — proxy media bytes through the server instead
known-working-patterns: npm-bundled rendering libraries that draw to canvas/WebGL/SVG with no string-to-code (e.g. canvas chart libs, data-driven 3D)
map libraries whose tiles load as elements (img-src is permissive) and that npm-bundle cleanly
web-component / view libraries that template without eval or new Function()
Media played from server-proxied same-origin bytes (data: URL or embedded resource) triggered by a user gesture

apphub.name: AppHub (custom MCP host)

server-initiated.notes: AppHub fulfils sampling/createMessage by bridging to its own agent SDK; its MCP client declares capabilities.sampling and answers CreateMessageRequestSchema. Downstream servers must still use a stateful transport. Elicitation not yet implemented.

bundling.notes: Full browser environment. Minimal sandbox. Both bundled and CDN approaches work.

port-config.notes: AppHub proxies MCP tool calls to downstream servers on their HTTP ports (3xxx).

known-working-patterns: Anything that works in a standard browser

standalone.name: basic-host (ext-apps reference)

server-initiated.notes: basic-host capability for server-initiated requests not validated firsthand. Treat as unavailable until probed (see sampling.md cross-host validation). Validate over stdio, which is inherently bidirectional.

bundling.notes: Most permissive. HTTP only (localhost:8080). No TLS.

known-working-patterns: Anything that works in a standard browser

copilothub.name: CopilotHub (GitHub Copilot SDK host)

csp.notes: No CSP injected into the iframe. UIs run in srcdoc with a base theme style only.

sandbox.notes: iframe sandbox = 'allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-popups allow-popups-to-escape-sandbox'. Browser/OS consent still applies to device APIs.

server-initiated.notes: CopilotHub fulfils sampling/createMessage by bridging to the GitHub Copilot SDK (sampleViaCopilot) — its MCP client declares capabilities.sampling and answers CreateMessageRequestSchema, including agentic sampling. No ElicitInputRequestSchema handler, so elicitation is not implemented. Downstream servers must still use a stateful transport. See copilot-sdk-host.md.

bundling.notes: Full browser environment served over TLS (port 4021). Both bundled and CDN approaches work.

port-config.notes: HTTPS only on 4021. Connects to downstream servers on their TLS ports (https://localhost:<HTTP+1000>/mcp), Streamable HTTP with SSE fallback.

authoring.notes: Custom host: feeds MCP servers into client.createSession({ mcpServers }) for the agent loop AND keeps an MCP Client proxy for resources/read, splash, status, and the sampling bridge.

known-working-patterns: Anything that works in a standard browser
Server->host sampling (sampling/createMessage) via the Copilot SDK bridge

notes: Only hosts that have been directly validated are listed. Other hosts (e.g. cloud chat assistants that render MCP App UIs) are generally more permissive than VS Code — but add a host entry here only after validating its capabilities firsthand via the HIT process in hit-process.md.
